title: Freedom of Information Act Request BWC & Dash Camera Footage Kyle Hennessey

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the The Missouri Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

Freedom of Information Act Request — Body Camera & Dash Camera Footage
Case Number: SLCPD-21-030652

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act and applicable state public records laws, I request copies of the following records:

INCIDENT DETAILS
  Date/Time:  2021-03-06 at 14:15
  Location:   St. Louis County, Missouri (Florissant area)
  Suspect:    Kyle J. Hennessey
  Charges:    Failure to Stop for Law Enforcement — Felony (RSMo 575.150), Assault of a Law Enforcement Officer (Second Degree), Operating a Motor Vehicle Without a Valid License, Property Damage (struck patrol vehicle)
  Case #:     SLCPD-21-030652

RECORDS REQUESTED

1. ALL body-worn camera (BWC) footage from each of the following officers and any additional officers who responded to or were present during this incident:

  • Deputy Aaron Lindqvist Badge #ST-0892 (Pursuing officer)
  • Deputy Renee Okafor Badge #ST-1044 (Secondary unit, spike strip deployment)

2. ALL dash camera / in-car video footage from every patrol vehicle, marked or unmarked, involved in the pursuit or apprehension.

3. Any supplemental video including aerial/overhead, pole-mounted cameras, and surveillance footage retained as part of the investigation.

4. Audio dispatch recordings and radio communications from the initial call through the conclusion of the incident.

FORMAT: Please provide records in original digital format (MP4 for video, WAV/MP3 for audio). Electronic delivery via secure download preferred.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Brandon Martin
